clause.title: Emergency access doors in enclosed lift shafts

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Where lifts are installed in enclosed lift shafts, having a distance between normal landing entrances greater than 11 m, emergency access doors must be provided and constructed as follows:

ol.li: The clear opening size of emergency doors must be not less than 600 mm wide x 2 m high.
Hinged doors must not open towards the interior of the lift shaft.
Doors must be self-closing and self-locking.
Doors must be marked on the landing side with the following words in letters not less than 35 mm high:DANGER LIFTWELL ACCESSKEEP FURNITURE AND FIXTURES CLEAR
Doors from the landing side must only be openable by a tool.
Each emergency door must be provided with a positive breaking electrical contact, wired into the control circuit to prevent movement of the lift until the emergency door is both closed and locked.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Emergency egress from the lift car must be provided in single enclosed lift shafts where—

ol.li: the vertical distance between the lift car sill and the landing door head is less than 600 mm; and
the counterweight is resting on its fully compressed buffer.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Emergency egress required by (2) must be in the form of an interlocked door with clear opening dimensions not less than 600 mm x 600 mm, accessible from the lift car entrance or the lift car roof (where the door is located in the wall of the lift shaft).
